programmes.show_description: The talented tattoo artists are back to revamp the nation's embarrassing and offensive body art, starting with a bad chat-up line tattoo and an unusual nipple inking.
Greg Davies takes five comics to task on their efforts to complete pointless tasks. Jo Brand and David Baddiel take time out to enjoy a sandwich.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett take on Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf. Adam Riches joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The Vitamin Shoppe's CEO, Sharon M Leite, goes undercover to learn how to grow her brand and experience how operational changes and innovations are impacting the front lines.
Laurent 'LT' Therivel, a Marine veteran who started as CEO of mobile network UScellular less than a year before going undercover, wants to see how his new brand is growing.
In Devon, Michelle and Shaun's confusing 'classical' evening competes with Amber and Lee's Mexican fiesta, and Mark and Adrian's European theme, in a week of fun, games and dancing.
In Bournemouth, Jackie and Richard's evening features some celebrity name-dropping. There's more bragging than cooking for Ross and Hanne. And Gail and Nigel take a risk: will it pay off?
...Selling: Rob and Julie's design choices have lowered the value of their cosy lakeside home. Can Jonathan doll up the place? Will Drew persuade Rob splash the cash?
The One with All the Boxing: Former boxing champ Jordan plans a fight-night wedding in front of thousands, while his bride-to-be Danielle has dreams of a feminine and intimate day.
The One with the Zip Wire: Thrill-seeker Lee plans an extreme wedding for his unadventurous bride Aimee. Will their big day have the wow factor, or will it crash to the ground?
Stephen J Cloobeck, CEO of hotel firm Diamond Resorts International, is the first boss to go undercover in his business for a second time, as he visits Pacific Monarch Resorts.
Steve Greenbaum, co-founder and CEO of PostNet, which helps small businesses with everything from printing to design, goes undercover to see how well his firm is functioning.
Zayna Flaming Grill (Part 1/2): Gordon makes the first of two visits to Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant in Redondo Beach owned by Faye and her niece Brenda.
Zayna Flaming Grill (Part 2/2): Gordon returns to California for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill. Can he inject flavour into the tired menu?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Daniel and Vanessa get hands-on to lift their lacklustre home into a sale-ready house. Can Drew balance glitz and glam with a macho 'man cave'?
Man Buns It Has Been Fun: The crew celebrate their final night out, and Daisy and Colin try to soak up every moment of it. Then everyone says their goodbyes.
David Baddiel steers a steamroller towards a representation of his childhood sweetheart, in the hope of impressing Taskmaster Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Cariad Lloyd take on Alan Carr and Kevin Bridges. Elis James and John Robins join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Congratulations: Adam has finally been recognised for his musical talent. But it's overshadowed when Jonny arrives home and announces he's got married.
The Pyjamas: Adam has been staying at Mum and Dad's all week, but with Mum fussing over him so much, he's started behaving like a little boy. And Jim passes his driving test.
Respectable dad Stephen wants to see the back of a crude tattoo he had done 20 years ago. Tattoo-mad ex-solider Rob's phallic self-portrait is a step too far.
David Baddiel steers a steamroller towards a representation of his childhood sweetheart, in the hope of impressing Taskmaster Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
In south London, there are feeding faux pas, raunchy revelations and kitchen catastrophes, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In the West Country, Charlotte and Miguel's Venezuelan menu competes with Pansy and Rob's sophisticated fare and Elle and Dave's organic, wheat-free feast.
The One in the Spanish Sea: Carl aims to marry Rebecca on the Med but she has expectations for a high-class affair. Will Carl's plans make a splash and make Rebecca swoon with delight?
The One with All the Pigs: Billy believes the sty's the limit as he plans a pig-themed wedding involving a world-record parade of porkers. Will bride Steph kick up a stink at Billy's plans?
The Aussie Soap One: Australian-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated.
The One Under Edinburgh: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. Will Jack's concept be a screaming success?
The Big Ibizan Zoo One: Sonny is a party fanatic. How will his bride Jazmin greet his plans for a zoo-themed Ibizan beach party wedding with everyone covered in animal-print body paint?
Colie Edison created every element of the bowling giant Bowlero. Now she goes undercover to find out how the brand is working for staff and customers.
Mayor Adrian Perkins of Shreveport, Louisiana, is the city's second youngest mayor and in charge of 3000 employees. He goes undercover, hoping to change the city's fortunes.
Zayna Flaming Grill (Part 2/2): Gordon returns to California for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill. Can he inject flavour into the tired menu?
Bella Luna: Gordon visits Italian family restaurant Bella Luna, which is struggling with an inexperienced chef and an owner who won't acknowledge the eatery's flaws.
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he's helped: La Galleria 33 in Boston; The Olde Hitching Post in Massachusetts; and the Prohibition Grille in Washington.
Roosevelt Inn: Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the owner of the Roosevelt Inn in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho is busy cracking jokes and playing dress up. Can Gordon help?
Cambridge Hotel: Gordon visits Cambridge, NY, where ex-military man John Imhof runs the Cambridge Hotel like a dictatorship, alienating his staff and guests.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Kathy Burke and Roisin Conaty take on Johnny Vegas and Bob Mortimer. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Vic Reeves take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley does the numbers.
An extended edition of the show with Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock, Jon Richardson and guests Russell Kane, Mark Watson, Louise Redknapp and Example tackling polls and opinions.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy statistics quiz, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and guests Johnny Vegas, Jennifer Metcalfe, Joe Lycett and Joey Essex.
